items.title: Taylor ziet zijn trainer Sarri bommetje droppen bij Lazio
items.summary: Het is onrustig bij Lazio. Na afloop van de remise tegen laagvlieger Lecce dropte trainer Maurizio Sarri een bommetje. Hij is duidelijk ontevreden over de clubleiding.

items.title: Mbappé draagt geslaagde Panenka op aan onfortuinlijke Díaz
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé besliste Villarreal – Real Madrid zaterdagavond in het voordeel van de bezoekers. De Franse aanvaller opende de score en maakte vlak voor tijd 0-2 uit een strafschop. De manier van benutten – een Panenka – deed ergens aan denken. Mbappé bevestigde na afloop dat hij het voor Brahim Díaz deed.

items.title: Tjaronn Chery hoeft zich niet te bewijzen: 'Mensen mogen zeiken'
items.summary: NEC kent een seizoen waarin zelfs slechte wedstrijden worden gewonnen. De ploeg van trainer Dick Schreuder was zichzelf niet tegen PEC Zwolle, maar won wel met 2-1. Met dank aan good old Tjaronn Chery (37).

items.title: Australian Open LIVE! Home hope De Minaur takes on showman Bublik
Reed holds big lead in Dubai as McIlroy chases fast finish LIVE!
Liverpool gekraakt na 'wisselblunder: 'Verbijsterend dat zoiets nog kan gebeuren'
items.summary: Een laat doorgevoerde wissel bij Liverpool maakt veel los in Engeland. De ploeg van Arne Slot verloor op bezoek bij Bournemouth . Een van de tegengoals kwam doordat Liverpool zeven minuten lang met tien man speelde.

items.title: Scheffler and teenager Brown one back at tight American Express
items.summary: World No 1 Scottie Scheffler and 18-year-old Blades Brown are one shot off leader Si Woo Kim on a congested leaderboard after three rounds of The American Express.

items.title: Groeiende frustraties in De Grolsch Veste
items.summary: FC Twente is met FC Groningen de ploeg met de minste tegengoals in de Eredivisie, maar over die statistiek heeft vrijwel niemand het in Enschede. Het probleem zit aan de andere kant, zo bleek tegen Excelsior (0-0) maar weer eens. ‘Je wordt er moedeloos van.’

items.title: Sabalenka eclipses Mboko but Jovic, 18, now stands in her Australian Open path
items.summary: Aryna Sabalenka fought off one teenage challenger but finds another in her path to the Australian Open semi-finals.

items.title: Improved Alcaraz serve carries him into Australian Open quarter-finals
items.summary: Carlos Alcaraz equalled his best run at the Australian Open by beating Tommy Paul in straight sets to reach the quarter-finals.

items.title: Slot: Liverpool ran out of energy in last-gasp loss
items.summary: Arne Slot said Liverpool "ran out of energy" in their 3-2 defeat to Bournemouth and said they had only themselves to blame for more dropped points in the Premier League.

items.title: Rodman marks 'fresh start' in scoring U.S. return
items.summary: USWNT coach Emma Hayes heralded a "fresh start" for Trinity Rodman after the forward scored in just her second match since the 2024 Olympics in a 6-0 rout of Paraguay.

items.title: 'Best in the world' Mbappé and Vini put Madrid top
items.summary: Coach Alvaro Arbeloa praised Kylian Mbappé and Vinícius Júnior as "the best players in the world" after the pair inspired Real Madrid to a 2-0 win at Villarreal.

items.title: Wrexham move into playoff spot with dramatic win
items.summary: Wrexham's bid for a fourth straight promotion and a place in the Premier League is gaining steam.

items.title: VAR review: Why Man City should have been given penalty vs. Wolves
items.summary: Referee Farai Hallam stuck with his original decision to not call a handball and penalty for Manchester City. Was it the right call?

items.title: Arne Slot says Liverpool ‘ran out of energy’ in defeat at Bournemouth
items.summary: Head coach highlights away game in Champions League Slot questions fixture list after difficult away trip Arne Slot conceded his side ran out of steam in defeat at Bournemouth, after Amine Adli’s 95th-minute winner condemned Liverpool to a first loss since November. Liverpool pulled level from 2-0 down late on courtesy of Dominik Szoboszlai’s sensational free-kick, but Bournemouth responded impressively and Adli struck a winner from a long throw with almost the last kick. The Liverpool head coach felt the referee, Michael Salisbury, should have played more second-half stoppage time taking in substitutions and video assistant referee checks but admitted he feared a Bournemouth winner. “I think it is safe to say they could have scored 3-2 a little bit earlier,” Slot said, alluding to chances for the Bournemouth pair Evanilson and Ryan Christie. “A few of our players ran out of energy and I cannot even criticise them for that because two days ago [three] we had to play an away game. We’re the only team that played in the Champions League that has two games in between. Continue reading...

items.title: Carrick has nailed quick fixes at Manchester United but is he more than new Solskjær? | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Derby victory was undeniably impressive but how will club assess caretaker manager’s suitability to permanent job? The problem Manchester United have – after 13 years and seven managers of failure – is that for whatever action they take now, there is a bad precedent. Keep Michael Carrick on, and it’s just another Ole Gunnar Solskjær situation. But replace him and, for almost whoever they appoint – be it a Premier League veteran, foreign maestro, renowned past-his-best winner, Red Bull-adjacent gegenpresser, austere Dutchman or Portuguese ideologue – they have done it before and it hasn’t worked. It’s almost like the biggest problem at the club isn’t the manager. Carrick’s start was undeniably impressive. There was pace and zip and creativity. The relief of players being released from the 3-4-2-1 was akin to one of those videos of cows being allowed back into the pasture after being kept in a barn over the winter. Who could possibly have predicted that Amad Diallo would excel as a right-sided forward, or that Bruno Fernandes might thrive as a No 10? United didn’t just beat Manchester City 2-0; they hammered them. Continue reading...

items.title: Adli's stoppage-time winner ends Liverpool unbeaten run in thriller
items.summary: Amine Adli scores a stoppage-time winner as Bournemouth end Liverpool's 13-game unbeaten run in a thrilling match at Vitality Stadium.

items.title: Bowen leads West Ham to important win over Sunderland
items.summary: A fantastic first-half performance helps West Ham blow away Sunderland and move to within two points of 17th-placed Nottingham Forest and safety.

items.title: Late Romero header rescues draw for Tottenham at Burnley
items.summary: Cristian Romero rescues a point for Tottenham Hotspur late on just as under-pressure head coach Thomas Frank faced further embarrassment against struggling Burnley at Turf Moor.
